data.title: Ferguson to Issue First Quarter Results And Host Conference Call on May 5, 2026
data.description:
NEWPORT NEWS, Va.--(BUSINESS WIRE)--April 21, 2026--

Ferguson Enterprises Inc. (; ) announces today that it will issue its first quarter results on Tuesday, May 5, 2026. The results will be available on Ferguson's website at corporate.ferguson.com at 6:45 a.m. ET/11:45 a.m. BST.

A conference call and webcast of the analyst and investor presentation will be broadcast at 8:30 a.m. ET/1:30 p.m. BST on the same day. Participants can register for the webcast at corporate.ferguson.com.

A slide presentation that accompanies the event will be available 15 minutes prior to the start time at corporate.ferguson.com on the Events, Results and Reports page under the Investors tab. An archived version of the webcast and slide presentation will be available for 12 months after the live event.

About Ferguson

Ferguson (; ) is North America's largest value-added distributor of essential water and air solutions, serving specialized professionals in our $340B residential and non-residential construction markets. We help make our customers' complex projects simple, successful and sustainable by providing expertise and a wide range of products and services from plumbing, HVAC, appliances, and lighting to PVF, water and wastewater solutions, and more. Headquartered in Newport News, Va., Ferguson has sales of $31.3 billion (CY'25) and approximately 35,000 associates in over 1,700 locations. For more information, please visit corporate.ferguson.com.

View source version on businesswire.com: https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20260421398727/en/

data.title: Eaton to announce first quarter 2026 earnings on May 5, 2026
data.description:
DUBLIN--(BUSINESS WIRE)--April 21, 2026--

Intelligent power management company Eaton will announce first quarter 2026 earnings on Tuesday, May 5, 2026, before the opening of the New York Stock Exchange. The company will host a conference call at 11 a.m. Eastern time that day to discuss first quarter 2026 earnings results.

The conference call will be available through a live webcast that can be accessed at Eaton.com/investor under "Presentations." The call replay and news release will also be available at the same link.

Eaton is an intelligent power management company dedicated to protecting the environment and improving the quality of life for people everywhere. We make products for the data center, utility, industrial, commercial and institutional, machine building, residential, aerospace and mobility markets. We are guided by our commitment to do business right, to operate sustainably and to help our customers manage power — today and well into the future. By capitalizing on the global growth trends of electrification and digitalization, we're helping to solve the world's most urgent power management challenges and building a more sustainable society for people today and generations to come.

Founded in 1911, Eaton has continuously evolved to meet the changing and expanding needs of our stakeholders. With revenues of $27.4 billion in 2025, the company serves customers in 180 countries. For more information, visit www.eaton.com. Follow us on LinkedIn.

View source version on businesswire.com: https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20260421817432/en/

data.title: Pizza Hut Relaunches Hut Rewards Program With New Perks
data.description:
WASHINGTON (dpa-AFX) - Pizza Hut, owned by Yum! Brands, Inc. (YUM), on Tuesday said it has relaunched its Hut Rewards program, positioning it as a next-generation membership platform focused on ongoing value, exclusive access and enhanced customer experiences.

The revamped program allows members to earn points on eligible purchases, progress faster through challenges and bonus opportunities, and access an expanded rewards catalog alongside member-only perks.

The company said the initiative aims to go beyond traditional points-based systems by offering continuous engagement, including exclusive experiences tied to cultural events.

Its recent March Madness campaign showcased the platform's capabilities, featuring in-app merchandise drops for members, including a limited-edition Space Jam collaboration collection, that sold out.

The company said customers can join Hut Rewards for free via the Pizza Hut app or online, earning and redeeming points on digital orders while gaining access to exclusive member-first experiences.

Yum! Brands shares closed at $161.82 on Monday, down 0.60%.

data.title: Halliburton posts higher first-quarter profit
data.description:
Halliburton reported a rise in first-quarter profit on Tuesday, as steady demand in Latin America and European markets helped offset a slowdown in activity in the Middle East due to the Iran war.

The U.S. oilfield services provider said net income attributable to the company was $461 million, or 55 cents per share, for the three months ended March 31, compared with $204 million, or 24 cents per share, a year earlier.

data.title: HSFO softens, Russian supplies to stay high
data.description:
Asia's high sulphur fuel oil (HSFO) market extended declines on Tuesday, with market structure narrowing sharply in backwardation at the prompt months.

The spot market is still seeing adequate prompt supplies, with Russian arrivals staying high into the month so far following the extended sanctions waiver.

Regional supplies also remained ample for now, according to trade sources.

Timespreads sold off on Tuesday, while Brent-basis cracks slipped back into discounts from premiums. The 380-cst HSFO crack closed at ICE Brent futures minus 30 cents a barrel, LSEG data showed at the Asia close. (FO380BRTCKMc1)

Meanwhile, the very low sulphur fuel oil (VLSFO) market held its ground as blending components remained limited, some sources said.

Marine fuel blenders face tighter supply of heavy sweet barrels for blending due to competition from refiners for the same oil after the Iran war disrupted sour crude imports, said sources.

Spot premiums for VLSFO held little changed from the previous day, though cracks (LFO05SGBRTCMc1) fell below $9 a barrel premiums, LSEG data showed.

REFINERY UPDATES

- The fluid catalytic cracker of Venezuela's second-largest refinery, the 310,000-barrel-per-day Cardon, was restarting on Monday after a power outage affected the entire refinery last week, two workers from the facility said.

OTHER NEWS

- Oil prices fell on Tuesday, reversing the previous session's gains, on expectations that peace talks between the U.S. and Iran will take place this week and lead to more supply flowing from the key Middle East producing region.

- Russian oil sales to India are set to remain near record highs in April and May following a new U.S. sanctions waiver, and refiners have already secured much of their supply needs via non-sanctioned entities and vessels, traders said and data showed on Tuesday.

- China will lower domestic retail gasoline and diesel price caps from Tuesday night, marking its first cut this year as global oil prices retreated from their peaks of the Iran war.

- Soaring oil prices in the wake of the U.S.-Israeli war on Iran are driving renewed demand for biofuels as the need to tackle a fossil fuel shortage outweighs concerns that using crops for fuel will drive up food prices.

WINDOW TRADES

- 180-cst HSFO: No trade.

- 380-cst HSFO: Two trades.

- 0.5% VLSFO: One trade.

data.title: Canada's Goodfood Market Q2 net sales fall 26%, net loss widens
data.description:
Overview

  • Canada meal solutions provider's fiscal Q2 net sales fell 26% yr/yr amid CFIA suspension

  • Gross margin dropped 12 percentage points due to higher costs from CFIA suspension

  • Net loss widened to C$6.8 mln, impacted by lower sales and reorganization costs

Outlook

  • Company is focused on improving unit economics and generating consistent cash flow

  • Goodfood plans selective expansion into adjacent food categories to support growth

  • Management remains focused on strengthening the balance sheet and maintaining capital flexibility

Result Drivers

  • CFIA SUSPENSION - Temporary CFIA license suspension reduced sales and increased costs, impacting gross margin and net loss

  • HIGHER COSTS - Gross margin fell due to higher shipping, labour and production costs mainly driven by the temporary CFIA suspension and lower fixed cost absorption

  • REORGANIZATION COSTS - Employee termination and other reorganization costs contributed to wider net loss

data.title: Space investment in first quarter hits record as SpaceX IPO buzz lifts sector
data.description:
Key points:
  • Average deal size nearly doubles, led by Saronic's $1.75 billion funding round
  • North America dominates funding, Europe and Asia also see strong investment growth
  • Capital increasingly targets in-space infrastructure like stations, data centers

By Akash Sriram

Global investment in space companies surged to a record in the first quarter of 2026, driven by larger late-stage financing and growing investor enthusiasm over SpaceX's public market debut, data from investment firm Seraphim Space showed on Tuesday.

Funding reached $7.95 billion during the quarter, nearly double the $3.93 billion in the previous three-month period, pushing the trailing 12-month investment to an all-time high of $18.8 billion.

Deal count also rose to 159 transactions, bringing the annual total to a record 654.

The increase in capital deployment was largely attributed to bigger cheque sizes rather than a sharp increase in deal volume, with average deal size climbing to $68 million from $35.1 million in fourth quarter. The largest transaction was U.S.-based Saronic's $1.75 billion round, one of the biggest space financings on record, the report said.

"The market today definitely feels 'risk-on' with capital moving quickly into perceived category leaders," said Lucas Bishop, investment associate at Seraphim Space, pointing to a convergence of tailwinds, including defense spending, renewed lunar ambitions and investor anticipation around SpaceX's IPO.

A SpaceX IPO could provide a landmark liquidity event for early investors and employees, while also creating a valuation benchmark, improving exit visibility for venture-backed space companies. Elon Musk's rocket maker ‌will host an analyst day ​on Tuesday, Reuters reported earlier this month.

North America accounted for roughly 70% of total funding in the first quarter, while Europe posted its strongest performance since 2022 and Asia contributed more than $1.2 billion.

Notably, investment has shifted beyond traditional satellite communications, with significantly more capital flowing into emerging segments such as in-space infrastructure, including space stations and data centers, reflecting a broadening of the sector's addressable market.

Recent developments have also highlighted continued momentum in satellite connectivity, with Amazon saying last week it would acquire Globalstar for $11.6 billion.

data.title: Sow Good to Acquire Nachu Graphite Project Holders From Ryzon in AUD150 Million Stock Deal
data.description:
Sow Good entered into a Share Purchase Agreement to acquire 100% of Uranex Tanzania and Magnis Technologies (Tanzania), which own the Nachu Graphite Project, from Ryzon. The AUD150 million (about US$107 million) consideration will be paid entirely in Sow Good common stock, adjusted for net debt and certain taxes, equating to approximately 334.2 million shares (or about 22.28 million after a 15-to-1 reverse split). A portion equal to AUD15 million will be held back as escrow via contingent value rights. Closing is subject to shareholder consent, Tanzanian regulatory approvals, Nasdaq listing approval of the shares, and other customary conditions, with a sunset date of Oct 15, 2026.

Agreement details:

  • Agreement type: Share Purchase Agreement for 100% acquisition of Uranex Tanzania and Magnis Technologies (Tanzania)
  • Counterparty: Ryzon Materials and sellers
  • Signed / Effective: Apr 20 2026 / same
  • Duration / Termination: Until closing; Sunset Oct 15 2026
  • Reason: Acquire Nachu Graphite Project to expand graphite supply and resources

Original SEC Filing: Sow Good Inc. [ SOWG ] - 8-K - Apr. 21, 2026

Disclaimer

data.title: GE Aerospace Revenue Rises as Orders Surge
data.description:
By Nicholas G. Miller

GE Aerospace posted higher first-quarter revenue on surging orders amid strong air travel and military demand.

The company reported net income from continuing operations of $1.93 billion, or $1.83 a share, compared with $1.97 billion, or $1.83 a share, the year prior.

Adjusted earnings were $1.86 a share. Analysts polled by FactSet had expected $1.60 a share.

Revenue rose 25% to $12.39 billion. Orders increased 87% to $23 billion. Commercial engines and services adjusted revenue was up 34%.

The company maintained its prior guidance, but said that it was trending toward the high end of its range. It expects fiscal-year adjusted earnings of $7.10 to $7.40 a share. Analysts see $7.49 a share.

Write to Nicholas G. Miller at nicholas.miller@wsj.com.

data.title: 3M posts Q1 revenue $6.03B, adjusted EPS $2.14 and adjusted operating margin 23.8%
data.description:
3M reported first-quarter 2026 results with GAAP revenue of $6.03 billion and GAAP operating margin of 23.2%. Adjusted results showed sales of $6.00 billion, adjusted operating margin of 23.8% and adjusted diluted EPS of $2.14. The company generated $574 million of operating cash flow and $541 million of adjusted free cash flow while reiterating full-year 2026 guidance.

Financial Highlights

  • Net sales (GAAP): $6,030 million for Q1 2026 (up 1.3% year‑on‑year).
  • Adjusted net sales (non‑GAAP): $6,003 million for Q1 2026 (adjusted sales change +3.9% YoY).
  • Operating income (GAAP): $1,397 million; GAAP operating margin 23.2% (up 230 bps YoY).
  • Adjusted operating income margin (non‑GAAP): 23.8% (up 30 bps YoY).
  • Adjusted diluted EPS (non‑GAAP): $2.14 (up 14% YoY); GAAP diluted EPS: $1.23 (down 40% YoY).
  • Cash flow: Net cash provided by operating activities $574 million; adjusted free cash flow $541 million.

Business Highlights

  • Segment revenue mix: Reportable segments generated $5,909 million in sales with Safety and Industrial at $2,930 million, Transportation and Electronics $1,848 million, and Consumer $1,131 million.
  • Segment performance: Safety and Industrial reported organic growth (3.2%); Transportation and Electronics and Consumer showed mixed organic trends with Transportation roughly flat and Consumer slightly negative organic sales.
  • Geographic trends: Strong growth in China (4.4% organic) and Europe, Middle East & Africa benefited from translation gains; Americas organic sales declined.
  • Corporate actions and restructuring: Company continues transformation program (incurring transformation costs) and reflected reclassification of manufactured PFAS products and certain litigation items into Corporate for clearer segment reporting.
  • Capital allocation and shareholder returns: Returned $2.4 billion to shareholders via dividends and share repurchases in the quarter; purchases of PPE were $225 million.

Original SEC Filing: 3M CO [ MMM ] - 8-K - Apr. 21, 2026

Disclaimer

data.title: D.R. Horton Stock Rises on Earnings Beat. Have Home Builders Hit Bottom? — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Shaina Mishkin

As the housing market limps along, analysts have become less enthused about D.R. Horton, the nation's largest home builder by market capitalization. The worst headwinds could abate this year even if earnings are soft, one analyst says.

D.R. Horton is set to report second quarter earnings on Tuesday before the market opens. According to consensus estimates by FactSet, the builder will report $7.55 billion in revenue and $2.13 a share. That would be down from $7.7 billion in revenue and $2.58 earnings per share in the year-ago period.

Builders have seen a bumpy start to the year. Prospective buyers were whiplashed by mortgage rates, which fell briefly below 6% this winter before jumping as high as 6.6% in late March, according to Mortgage News Daily. They have since mellowed to a recent 6.3%.

The swoon came after President Donald Trump said Fannie Mae and Freddie Mac would buy mortgage bonds, while the jump followed the 10-year Treasury yield's move higher as a result of the war in Iran.

"I don't think demand has been completely annihilated because of the war, but I'm sure it's been impacted," says John Lovallo, a UBS analyst covering home builders. "Consumer confidence has absolutely been impacted."

Analysts have become progressively more cautious on D.R. Horton. In the quarter ended March 31, 29% of analysts covering the stock rated it Buy, down from 41% one year prior, according to FactSet. The stock closed at $153.34 on Monday, its highest close since March 2, according to Dow Jones Market Data. Shares are up 6.5% this year.

Investors are most focused on finding a bottom for builders' margins, Lovallo says. Consensus estimates for D.R. Horton foresee a 19.1% home sales gross margin, narrower than the 21.8% the builder reported in the same quarter in 2025. Estimates are for 19,941 deliveries and 23,583 new orders.

Investors should keep their eyes on guidance, which could influence expectations for the company's third quarter. Analysts as of Monday afternoon expect 22,849 deliveries in the third quarter with a roughly 18% home sales gross margin.

If other builder earnings are any guide, investors watching D.R. Horton and PulteGroup, which is scheduled to report results on Thursday, can expect "relatively soft quarters" and lower outlooks, Lovallo says. That "doesn't sound like a great setup," he says. But "there's a view out there that we're getting closer to a bottom in the cycle," he adds.

Lovallo has a Buy on both stocks, he says. "If folks believe that the margins are going to bottom this year and consumer confidence will bottom this year — which I hope it does, because we're running at very low levels — then this group becomes very exciting," he says.

Write to Shaina Mishkin at shaina.mishkin@dowjones.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: US Reports Progress In Combating HIV Epidemic Globally
data.description:
WASHINGTON (dpa-AFX) - The U.S. Government has reported tremendous progress in reducing mother-to-child transmission of HIV, and in combating the epidemic as a whole across the world.

The U.S. Department of State has released PEPFAR program data covering the period of July 1, 2025, to September 31, 2025.

U.S. Government-supported programs provided anti-retroviral treatment for 20.6 million people living with HIV in more than 50 countries, stable from FY 2024 Q4.

PEPFAR initiated 103,000 pregnant and breastfeeding women to start pre-exposure prophylaxis to protect them and their babies from HIV-more than doubling the 43,000 pregnant and breastfeeding women on pre-exposure prophylaxis just one year ago.

The number of children on HIV treatment declined from 643,627 in 2022 to 508,703 in 2025 - representing year-over-year declines of 7 percent, 7 percent , and 9 percent respectively. This reflects tremendous progress in reducing mother-to-child transmission consistent with historical trends, according to a fact sheet published by the Department of State.

These declines are expected to continue, particularly with U.S. Government funding of Lenacapavir, which can further prevent mother-to-child transmission.

Positive HIV tests declined from 1,693,349 in 2022 to 1,136,488 in 2025 - representing year-over-year declines of 14 percent, 12 percent, and 11 percent respectively - reflecting progress in combating the HIV epidemic consistent with historical trends.

The State Department said this data does not reflect the impact of bilateral MOUs or the broader strategies being implemented under the America First Global Health Strategy.

data.title: Safran Faces More Challenging Aftermarket Business — Market Talk
data.description: French aerospace group Safran faces a more challenging aftermarket business, Jefferies analysts say. The short-lived reopening of the Strait of Hormuz, a vital waterway for fuel trade, gave some respite to civil aerospace groups, but the situation is likely to remain volatile and impact air traffic demand, the analysts say. Safran's first-quarter results, due April 23, should be solid and not impacted by the Middle East conflict, they say. Jefferies downgrades its rating of Safran stock to hold from buy, and cuts its target price to 310 euros from 350 euros. Shares are down 2.7% at 294.7 euros. (cristina.gallardo@wsj.com)
